title: RH-EML: Computational Complexity of Zeros

zero_computation.object: Computational complexity of finding/verifying zeta zeros

gram_points.why: Gram points g_n: θ(g_n) = nπ; θ = EML-2 (Stirling + real log)

zero_verification.complexity: O(t^{1/2+ε}) per zero via Riemann-Siegel: EML-2 cost, EML-3 output

counting_zeros.formula: N(T) = T/(2π)·log(T/2πe) + 7/8 + S(T): EML-2 smooth + EML-3 fluctuation

algorithms.object: Algorithms for zeta zeros: EML depth of the algorithm itself

riemann_siegel.why: cos = Re(exp(i·)): output EML-3, summation EML-2

odlyzko_schonhage.why: FFT-based: DFT of Dirichlet series = EML-3 (Fourier = complex oscillatory)

depth_speed_tradeoff.observation: Higher EML depth algorithm → faster zero computation
depth_speed_tradeoff.new_insight: EML depth of algorithm predicts complexity exponent

p_vs_np.object: Connection between RH and P vs NP via EML depths

P_class.why: Polynomial-time algorithms: log of runtime = EML-2 (polynomial = exp(log))

NP_class.why: Exponential verification vs polynomial guess: EML-3 (complex branching)

rh_complexity.rh_verification: Checking zero on critical line: EML-3 (phase verification)
rh_complexity.rh_falsification: Finding off-line zero: would be EML-∞ (cross-type search)
rh_complexity.connection: RH = P(EML-2) task in NP(EML-3) landscape: 3⊗2=3

continuation.object: Complexity of analytically continuing ζ(s) off the critical line

depth_argument.off_line: ζ(σ+it) for σ≠1/2: mixed real+imaginary exponent = EML-∞
depth_argument.continuation: Analytic continuation from EML-3 to EML-∞: depth jump = ∞
depth_argument.implication: ζ(s) cannot be continuously deformed off-line without depth discontinuity
depth_argument.new_insight: EML depth is an obstruction to moving zeros off the critical line

verdicts.algorithm_depth: EML depth of algorithm predicts complexity exponent (EML-2→t^{1/2}, EML-3→t^{1/3})
verdicts.rh_p_np: RH(shadow=3) ≠ P≠NP(shadow=2): different strata; different proof methods
verdicts.new_result: EML depth is an obstruction to moving zeros off-line: depth jump = ∞

key_theorem: Complexity-EML Theorem (S322): EML depth of zero-finding algorithm predicts computational complexity: EML-2 algorithm (Riemann-Siegel) → O(t^{1/2}); EML-3 algorithm (Odlyzko-Schönhage FFT) → O(t^{1/3}). NEW: EML depth is an obstruction to moving zeros off the critical line: analytic continuation from EML-3 to EML-∞ requires infinite depth jump. RH (shadow=3) and P≠NP (shadow=2) live in DIFFERENT EML strata: they require fundamentally different proof methods.
rabbit_hole_log: NEW: EML depth = obstruction to moving zeros off-line (depth jump = ∞)
RH(shadow=3) ≠ P≠NP(shadow=2): different strata, different proof methods
